Generic term for the player's level, as typically seen in role-playing games.
Generic display of the player's level, as typically seen in role-playing games. The current level (1, 2, 3 .. 39, 40) is inserted for {0}.
Generic display of the player's rank within a given faction, as typically seen in role-playing games. The current rank (0, 1, 2 .. 9) is inserted for {0}.
Generic display of the in-game day. The current day (1, 2, 3 .. 500, 501 ...) is inserted for {0}.
Generic display of a number of remaining days before an effect or an ongoing event runs out.

Generic term (short!) for the player's experience points, as typically seen in role-playing games.
Generic term for the players wealth, i.e. how much money is available.
Generic term for the player's reputation with a faction.

General term for Action Points the player can spend on doing certain actions.

SourceString: Використовуйте деталі з цієї книги, допоки вимога не буде виконана. Потрібні деталі позначені символом <ReqPart/>.
Comment: Description of a style requirement, asking the player to use sword parts from a specific book. An image is unserted for <ReqPart/>.

SourceString: Використовуйте цей матеріал, допоки вимога не буде виконана. Потрібний матеріал позначено символом <ReqMaterial/>.
Comment: Description of a style requirement, asking the player to use a specific material. An image is inserted for <ReqMaterial/>.

SourceString: Використовуйте матеріали пофарбовані цим барвником, допоки вимога не буде виконана. Потрібні матеріали позначено символом <ReqPigment/>.
Comment: Description of a style requirement, asking the player to use a specific pigment. An image is inserted for <ReqPigment/>.
Title of the gameplay-rating.
Description of the blade length bar's meaning

SourceString: Загальна довжина клинка, виміряна від основи до вістря.
Comment: Text inside tooltip, explaining the physical value of the blade length.
Title of the gameplay-rating.
Description of the weight bar's meaning

SourceString: Загальна вага меча. Легкий меч дозволяє рухатися швидше і менше втомлює, але більш важкий меч завдає сильнішого удару.
Comment: Text inside tooltip, explaining the physical value of the weight.
Title of the gameplay-rating.

SourceString: Від нахилу в сторону руків'я до вістря відповідно
Comment: Description of the balance bar's meaning

SourceString: Центр ваги — важлива властивість. Регулюйте його, змінюючи розподіл ваги між держаком і лезом.
Comment: Text inside tooltip, explaining the physical value of the balance.
Title of the gameplay-rating.

SourceString: Яка сила припадає на удар. Це можна регулювати, зміщуючи центр ваги або змінюючи розподіл маси. Маса, розташована поблизу центру ваги, поводитиметься інакше, ніж маса, направлена на вістря чи на голівку руків'я.
Comment: Text inside tooltip, explaining the physical value of the blade mass and mass distribution.
Title of the gameplay-rating.

SourceString: Плоске і широке лезо з гострим краєм ідеально підійде для різаного удару. Використовуйте кольори на екрані поперечного зрізу собі на користь.
Comment: Text inside tooltip, explaining the physical value of slashing and how to sharpen the edge.
Title of the gameplay-rating.

SourceString: Гостре вістря на вузькому або звуженому лезі зробить ваш меч ідеальним для колючих атак.
Comment: Text inside tooltip, explaining the physical value of piercing and how to apply abilities that are ideal for thrusting.

Title of the tapering skill window in management mode skill tree, referring the blade of the sword.

SourceString: The balance of the blade comes alive through its taper. Tapering is used to shape the handling characteristics of the blade and to optimise the weight distribution.
Comment: Text inside the tapering skill window in management mode skill tree, describing what the skill is about.

SourceString: Unlocks <ability>Profile Taper</> to make blades taper in width.
Comment: Text inside the tapering skill window in management mode skill tree, showing the player the exact ability that will be unlocked by learning this skill.

SourceString: Unlocks <ability>Distal Taper</> to make blades taper in thickness.
Comment: Text inside the tapering skill window in management mode skill tree, showing the player the exact ability that will be unlocked by learning this skill.
Title of the tapering skill window in management mode skill tree, referring the edge of a blade.

SourceString: The cross-section is a crucial piece of the puzzle in the making of blades. It greatly influences the intended use of the blade and its ability to cut and pierce.
Comment: Text inside the cross-section skill window in management mode skill tree, describing what the skill is about.
Text inside the cross-section skill window in management mode skill tree, showing the player the exact ability that will be unlocked by learning this skill.
Title of the crafting skill window in management mode skill tree, referring the hilt section of a sword.

SourceString: In the days of the old, specialised craftsmen created the stages of a blade – a luxury you don’t have in Eren Keep. You are making your own hilt parts, which is just as important as shaping the blade itself.
Comment: Text inside the crafting skill window in management mode skill tree, describing what the skill is about.
Text inside the crafting skill window in management mode skill tree, showing the player the exact ability that will be unlocked by learning this skill.
Title of a UI element on the way to the next skill point, referring to the players journey to become a better swordsmith.
Title of the main branching skill window in management mode skill tree.

SourceString: You are now worthy of calling yourself a swordsmith. Increase your skills to produce even more complex blades and make your forge flourish.
Comment: Text inside the main branching skill window in management mode skill tree, complimenting the player for come this far and going further to become an even better swordsmith.
Title of a skill that improves the ability to salvage resources from miscellaneous/scrap items.
Description of a skill that improves the ability to salvage resources from miscellaneous/scrap items.

SourceString: Unlocks various kinds of leather for crafting.
Comment: Description of unlocks the player gains by learning the main branching skill.
Description of fixed malus the player receives by learning the main branching skill. Daily upkeep is the cost the player has to pay each day for maintaining the forge.
Skill effect: the amount to be paid per day for the forge's upkeep is reduced by fixed value X.
Skill effect: the amount to be paid per day for the forge's upkeep is reduced by X percent.

Title of the strong hands skill window in management mode skill tree, referring to the players hands as a swordsmith getting stronger over time from working in the forge.

SourceString: The swinging of the hammer has strengthened your grip. With more strength in your hands, you can do more work during the day.
Comment: Text inside the strong hands skill window in management mode skill tree, describing what the skill is about.
Title of the negotiation skill window in management mode skill tree, referring to the players negotiating skills and how these affect the discounts the player gets at vendors, buying sword parts.

SourceString: You learn to better assess the people in Eren Keep and can use this to your advantage in negotiations.

Title of the patient client skill window in management mode skill tree, describing the earned trust and patience from customers, so the player gets the chance to have more time for each commission.

SourceString: Good things take time. Your clients know this too and are willing to give you a little more time to complete your work.
Comment: Text inside the patient client skill window in management mode skill tree, describing what the skill is about.

Comment: Text inside the patient client skill window in management mode skill tree, showing the player the exact advantage that will be unlocked by learning this skill.

Parts the player owns as single instances (rather than crafting recipes).
The guard is a swordpart that describes the metal part that protects the hand and is located between the handle and the blade.
Part of the guard of edged weapons consisting of a small block of metal, acting as a support for the shoulder of the blade and the base of the guard.
The quillon is an arm of the guard of a sword.
A connecting piece to connect e.g. two quillons.
A sword part in ring form, often attached to the quillon block.
Sword parts in the form of sweeps and loops, usally part of the guard in e.g. a rapier.
End caps to put on the end of quillons / arms.
The Grip acts as the main part of the handle. You hold the sword here.
Wrappings around the grip parts of the sword to further decorate.
An elevated piece, usually used on grips. Traditionally, this was achieved by putting a thick cord around the wooden grip piece before covering it with leather, resulting in a bump.
A pommel is a shaped protrusion at the end of a sword hilt, serves as a counter weight.
A pommel is a shaped protrusion at the end of a sword hilt, serves as a counter weight. Complex pommels can be modified further by attaching more parts onto it.
The part that is peened is the end of the tang (the part of the blade that is inside the guard, grip, and pommel) where it goes through the pommel. This is what seals the hilt shut.
Various items that do not fit into any other category, abbreviation for Miscellaneous. Feel free to use an abbreviation or a full word, we're not particularly space-limited here.
Various decorative parts that can be attached to the sword.
A ring found on some sword hilts that connects two grip parts, usually (but not always) decorative and made of metal.
A decorative object that can be attached to the sword, e.g. a coin, medal etc.

A rating of how hard a sword hits.

SourceString:
How hard the sword hits.

A sword hits hardest when it’s heavy and has a lot of mass near the point.

Comment: A rating of how fast a sword can be swung/redirected.

SourceString:
How swiftly the blade can be swung or redirected.

A sword’s speed is highest when the sword is light and has little mass near the point. A longer handle further improves this rating.

Comment: A rating of how easily a sword's hilt can be repositioned to defend or change stance.

SourceString:
How easily the hilt can be repositioned to defend or change stance.

A sword is most mobile when it’s light and has little weight in the hilt area.

Comment: A rating of how well a sword's cross-section of the sword can cut through a target.

SourceString:
How well the cross-section of the sword can cut through a target.

The steep edge angle of a wide and thin blade provides the best rating. A smooth cross-section is preferable – a strong central ridge can impede the cut.

Comment: A rating of how stiff a blade is, i.e. with how much force you can thrust before the blade starts to bend.

SourceString:
How fiercely the sword can thrust before it starts to bend.

This rating is improved by making your blade thicker, at the expense of more weight. Experiment with distal tapering, hollow cross-sections and fullers to keep the weight low.

Comment: A rating of how acute a sword's tip/point is.

SourceString:
How effective the sword’s point is at piercing targets.

An acute, gradual point is the ideal shape for this rating. This might take away from the sword’s Impact – experiment with the blade’s width, thickness and tapering to find the right balance.

SourceString: Someone had a little too much to drink. Reduces your AP and Knowledge.
You’re suffering, and so is your productivity. Reduces your AP and Toughness.
So... tired... Reduces your AP and Finesse.
A hearty meal has steeled you for the day ahead. Increases your AP and Toughness.
You’re crackling with energy, brimming with ideas. Increases your earned XP and Diplomacy.
Ugly rumours are spreading. Reduces your daily Commissions and Diplomacy.
Darkness seeps at the edges of your sight. Reduces your AP and all attributes.

Comment: Name of an in-game faction. The Crows are a loose bunch of mercenaries and scavengers who roam the wastelands and pick the loot off the dying world, or sell their sword to the highest bidder.

Name of an in-game faction. The Magisterium is a highly organized group of scholars who have made it their mission to restore the world's body of knowledge after the game's cataclysmic events by gathering and copying every book they can.
Name of an in-game faction. The Houndsburg League are a powerful and ruthless trading organization. They offer protection to their members, and extortion to those who refuse to join their umbrella.
